title: Sentinel Shield transaction journal entry
description: ONE line of the append-only transaction journal at <target>/.sentinel-shield/transaction-journal.jsonl. The journal is JSON Lines (JSONL): each line is an independent object conforming to this schema. Entries are appended (never rewritten) across every phase of a mutating install/sync/migrate/recover operation. Each entry chains to the previous one via 'prev' (the previous entry's 'hash'); 'hash' is a digest of the entry with its 'hash' field removed. Truncation and in-place tampering of any prefix break the chain and are rejected by scripts/recover-operation.sh --inspect. A keyless hash chain detects corruption and casual tampering; it is NOT tamper-PROOF against a full re-chaining rewrite. Entries carry NO secrets and NO file contents — only relative paths and short phase details.

schema_version.description: Journal-entry schema version. Fixed at '1' for this contract.

seq.description: 1-based monotonic sequence number, equal to this entry's line position in the journal.

ts.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp when the entry was appended.

operation.description: The mutating operation that emitted the entry.

pid.description: PID of the process that emitted the entry.

path.description: Project-relative path the entry concerns, or "" when the phase is not file-scoped. MUST be relative (no leading '/', no '..' segment): a tampered absolute/traversal path is rejected on inspect.

detail.description: Short human-readable phase detail. MUST NOT contain secrets or file contents.

prev.description: The 'hash' of the previous journal entry, or "" for the first entry. Links the chain.

hash.description: Digest of this entry with the 'hash' field removed (sha256 hex, or a 'cksum:<crc>' fallback when no sha256 tool is present). Recomputed on inspect to detect tampering.
